message:
Implement index orchestration and CLI integration

Add complete indexing pipeline connecting connectors, storage, and
search layers, plus CLI command integration for `cargo run -- index`.

IndexOptions struct:
Configuration for index operations passed from CLI:
- full: bool for full rebuild vs incremental (incremental not implemented
  yet, full always true for now)
- watch: bool to enable filesystem watching after initial scan (loops
  forever re-running index on file changes)
- db_path: SQLite database location (from CLI --db flag or config default)
- data_dir: Application data directory for Tantivy index (platform-
  specific via directories crate in future, hardcoded for now)

run_index function (main orchestration):
Entry point called from lib.rs Commands::Index handler:
1. Open SQLite storage at db_path (creates database, runs migrations if
   first run per storage::sqlite::SqliteStorage::open)
2. Open or create Tantivy index at data_dir/index/v1/ (index_dir helper
   computes versioned path per search::tantivy::index_dir)
3. Instantiate all six connectors in Box<dyn Connector> vector for
   polymorphic iteration (CodexConnector, ClineConnector, GeminiConnector,
   ClaudeCodeConnector, OpenCodeConnector, AmpConnector)
4. For each connector:
   - Call detect() to check if agent installed
   - Skip if not detected (no error, just continue to next—allows running
     index on system with only subset of agents installed)
   - Build ScanContext with data_root (same as opts.data_dir, passed to
     connector for history location resolution) and since_ts:None (always
     full scan for now)
   - Call scan(&ctx) to get Vec<NormalizedConversation> from agent logs
   - Call ingest_batch to insert conversations into SQLite and Tantivy
5. Commit Tantivy index (flush buffered documents to disk, make searchable
   via t_index.commit())
6. If watch enabled, enter filesystem watching loop (watch_sources with
   callback re-running run_index on changes)

ingest_batch helper:
For each NormalizedConversation:
- Call persist::persist_conversation to:
  1. Ensure agent exists in SQLite (ensure_agent upserts Agent row)
  2. Ensure workspace exists if conversation has workspace path
  3. Map NormalizedMessage.role strings to MessageRole enum (map_role
     helper: "user" -> User, "assistant"/"agent" -> Agent, "tool" -> Tool,
     "system" -> System, other -> Other(string))
  4. Build model::types::Conversation with mapped messages
  5. Insert to SQLite via insert_conversation_tree (transaction-wrapped
     batch insert of conversation + messages + snippets)
  6. Add to Tantivy via t_index.add_conversation (one Document per message)

persist::persist_conversation abstraction:
Bridges connector output (NormalizedConversation with string roles) to
storage input (Conversation with MessageRole enum) and search input
(Tantivy Documents). Responsibilities:
- Agent metadata: For now uses agent_slug for both slug and name,
  AgentKind::Cli for all (real implementations will differentiate Cli
  vs VsCode, extract name from detection evidence)
- Workspace handling: Calls ensure_workspace only if conversation has
  workspace path, passes None otherwise (avoids creating empty workspace
  rows)
- Message mapping: Iterates .messages with .map, builds new Message
  structs with:
  - idx from NormalizedMessage.idx (preserves order)
  - role from map_role (string -> enum conversion)
  - author, created_at, content copied directly
  - extra_json from NormalizedMessage.extra (preserves tool-specific
    metadata)
  - snippets empty Vec (snippet extraction not implemented in connectors
    yet, deferred to future)
- Conversation assembly: Creates Conversation with id:0 (placeholder,
  SQLite assigns real ID on insert), copies fields from NormalizedConversation,
  wraps mapped messages in .messages vec
- Dual writes: insert_conversation_tree returns conversation ID (currently
  unused), add_conversation doesn't return value (appends to Tantivy
  writer buffer)

watch_sources function (filesystem monitoring):
Uses notify crate to watch agent data directories for changes:
1. Create recommended_watcher with callback closure (invoked on fs events:
   file created, modified, deleted)
2. Callback executes provided function (closure captures run_index call
   with watch:false to avoid infinite recursion—watch triggers reindex,
   reindex doesn't re-watch)
3. Iterate watch_roots() to get list of agent history paths:
   - Codex: $CODEX_HOME or ~/.codex/sessions
   - Cline: VS Code globalStorage (Linux path, macOS not handled yet)
   - Gemini: ~/.gemini/tmp
   - Claude: ~/.claude/projects
4. watcher.watch(dir, RecursiveMode::Recursive) registers each root
   (Recursive descends into subdirectories, critical for Codex sessions/
   hierarchy)
5. Loop forever with 60-second sleep (prevents thread exit, watcher
   continues firing callbacks on changes until process killed)

watch_roots hardcoded paths:
Currently duplicates detection logic from connectors (e.g., ClineConnector::
storage_root uses same ~/.config/Code/User/globalStorage path). Future
refactoring: have connectors expose watch_paths() method returning
Vec<PathBuf> to DRY (per PLAN § 8.2 watch management).

CLI integration (lib.rs Commands::Index handler):
Replace stub tracing::info with actual indexer call:
```rust
Commands::Index { full } => {
    indexer::run_index(IndexOptions {
        full,
        watch: false,  // TODO: add --watch CLI flag
        db_path: cli.db.unwrap_or_else(|| default_db_path()),
        data_dir: default_data_dir(),
    })
}
```
Currently uses hardcoded default paths (db_path, data_dir) since config
module not implemented yet. Future: load from config.toml with CLI
overrides.

Import organization (lib.rs):
Add indexer::IndexOptions and all connector types (CodexConnector,
ClineConnector, etc.) to imports for run_index construction. Reformat
imports to group connectors module items together (Connector trait,
NormalizedConversation type, then connector structs).

Error handling:
All operations propagate errors via ? operator:
- Connector scan failures: continue to next connector (future: log
  warning, accumulate error count, display summary)
- Storage insert failures: abort (transaction rollback, Tantivy uncommitted
  so no partial state)
- Tantivy commit failure: abort (user sees error, can delete index
  directory and retry)

Testing manually:
```bash
cargo run -- index --full
# Expect: detects Codex/Cline/Gemini, scans conversations, inserts to
# SQLite, builds Tantivy index
# Verify: SQLite DB created at default path with agents/conversations/
# messages tables populated
# Verify: index/v1/ directory created with segment_* files
```

Known limitations (future enhancements):
- No progress reporting (silent during index, user doesn't know if frozen
  or processing—add indicatif::ProgressBar in future)
- No incremental scanning (opts.full ignored, always scans all files—
  implement ScanContext.since_ts filtering)
- No error recovery (one connector failure aborts entire index—implement
  per-connector try-catch with error accumulation)
- Watch mode untested (infinite loop difficult to test, needs Ctrl+C
  handling via tokio::signal::ctrl_c for graceful shutdown)
- Hardcoded paths (db_path, data_dir need config module for proper
  platform-specific defaults)

Related: PLAN_TO_MAKE_CODING_AGENT_SESSION_SEARCH.md § 8 (indexer/sync
flow), § 8.2 (incremental updates with notify), § 14.7 (indexer
orchestration), connectors/mod.rs (Connector trait), storage/sqlite.rs
(insert_conversation_tree), search/tantivy.rs (add_conversation, commit).
